subtasks.description: Query Salesforce system tables to extract licensed seat count, seat type distribution, assigned user count, and assignment date metadata across all license types. Join license allocation tables with active user records to compute utilization metrics.

Compare licensed seats vs actual assignments, calculate utilization percentage by license type, identify unutilized seat counts and costs, classify licenses as fully utilized, partially utilized, or unutilized. Generate actionable recommendations for seat reallocation or downgrade.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks required: (1) sql_gen to extract seat allocation and usage data from Salesforce system tables with multi-table joins—mid tier due to complexity of license type aggregation and user-seat mapping; (2) cross_recon to reconcile licensed vs actual and classify utilization posture with recommendations—mid tier as this involves multi-signal analysis (license types, assignment dates, utilization ratios) and decision generation. No policy_action needed as this is analysis/recommendation, not governance approval. No third data source, so no additional cross_recon. Kept to minimum: single query pull + single reconciliation analysis.

output.sql: SELECT cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_licensed_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS assigned_users, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) / NULLIF(MAX(cf.daily_active_seats), 0), 2) AS utilization_pct, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) - COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS unutilized_seats, MIN(cf.event_date) AS first_event_date, MAX(cf.event_date) AS last_event_date FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%salesforce%' GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.license_id ORDER BY unutilized_seats DESC, total_spend DESC
